label: Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning

node.label: Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning
node.definition: Task decomposition breaks a broad objective into ordered or partially ordered subgoals whose outputs, dependencies, and stopping conditions can be managed explicitly.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning is to connect Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* to downstream capabilities including PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning, Observe–plan–act agent loops.
examples: substrate for PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*.

dependencies.rationale: Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning uses Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning uses Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
